data.title: Equinor ASA: Ex. dividend fourth quarter 2024 today – OSE
data.description:
The shares in Equinor ASA (OSE: EQNR; NYSE: EQNR) will as from today be traded on the Oslo Stock Exchange exclusive the fourth quarter 2024 cash dividend as detailed below.

Ex. date: 15 May 2025

Dividend amount: 0.37

Announced currency: USD

This information is published in accordance with the requirements of the Continuing Obligations and is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to Section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

data.title: Market Chatter: Former Unisplendour Chair Gets Death Sentence Over Corruption, Embezzlement
data.description:
A Chinese court sentenced Zhao Weiguo, ex-chair of semiconductor company Unisplendour , to death with a two-year reprieve for corruption and embezzlement, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Zhao, who diverted company funds for personal gain and harmed a listed firm's interests, was fined 12 million yuan, according to the report.

The sentence could be commuted to life imprisonment if no further crimes are committed, Reuters wrote.

data.title: India's Muthoot Finance drops as Kotak flags near-term challenges
data.description:
** India's Muthoot Finance slumps 5.7% to 2,131 rupees

** Gold loan financier beat fourth-quarter profit estimates but Kotak analysts flagged near-term risks for the lender

** Reserve Bank of India (RBI) proposed a loan-to-value (LTV) ratio limit of 75% which would apply throughout the loan tenure and not just at origination

** A cut in LTV will pose a risk to margins and growth, says Kotak

** Moreover, with high growth, competition in the sector is likely to increase, Kotak says citing IIFL Finance resuming gold loans and Bajaj Finance's strong gold loan growth

** Brokerage downgrades MUTT to "reduce" from "add" with fair value at 2,275 rupees from 2,250 rupees

** Avg rating on MUTT at "buy"; median PT is 2,500 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock trims YTD gains to 0.4%

data.title: New Zealand Stocks Rise on Thursday
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.8% to close at 12,881 on Thursday, driven by gains in health technology stocks.

Heavyweights Fisher & Paykel and Ebos Group advanced 3.7% and 0.5%, respectively.

Other large-cap stocks also ended higher, including Auckland Intl Airport (1.3%), Mercury NZ (1.5%), Contact Energy (0.9%), and A2 Milk (1.3%).

Investor sentiment was further buoyed by a temporary halt in the trade war between the US and China, which eased worries of a global recession.

In domestic news, annual food inflation in New Zealand rose to 3.7% in April, the highest in fifteen months, from 3.5% in March.

data.title: Bridgestone Maintains Revenue, Net-Profit Views for 2025
CEZ proposes dividend at top of range, first-quarter profit above estimates
data.description:
Czech electricity producer CEZ proposed paying a 47 crown ($2.11) per share dividend on Thursday, amounting to 80% of last year's profit and below a previous payout of 52 crowns.

The dividend was at the top of the company's typical payout ratio of 60% to 80% of adjusted net profit. CEZ shares had closed on Wednesday at 1,194 crowns and have risen 30% in the past year.

CEZ earlier this year reported a 9% decrease in 2024 adjusted net profit to 31.8 billion crowns.

On Thursday, it confirmed its outlook for 2025 and expects an adjusted net profit of between 25 billion crowns and 29 billion crowns.

CEZ said its first-quarter adjusted net profit fell 6% to 12.7 billion crowns, affected by higher depreciation and amortisation. The average estimate in a Reuters poll had seen adjusted profit at 10.4 billion crowns in the quarter.

Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) rose 7% year-on-year in the quarter to 43 billion crowns.

CEZ slightly increased its full-year EBITDA outlook to between 127 billion crowns and 132 billion crowns in 2025, versus 137.5 billion crowns in 2024, as lower electricity prices weighed.

This year is likely the last in which CEZ will pay a windfall tax that was introduced after energy price spikes following Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

CEZ said it had pre-sold around two-thirds of its expected 2026 generation output at an average 94 euros per MWh, versus an average realised price of 120 euros to 125 euros assumed for 2025.

($1 = 22.2570 Czech crowns)

data.title: CTBC Bank Redeems Structured Bonds Early
data.description:
CTBC Financial unit CTBC Bank triggered an early redemption event on its nine-month USD knock out fixed coupon basket equity-linked structured bonds.

The bonds were repaid following a knock-out event, according to a Taiwan Exchange filing on Wednesday.

data.title: Rich Capital Completes Disposal of Subsidiary
data.description:
Rich Capital Holdings completed the disposal of its entire stake in wholly owned subsidiary, Rich Batam, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

In an earlier filing, the company had entered into an agreement with Yi Chengshu to dispose of the subsidiary for an aggregate consideration of SG$1.

Accordingly, Rich Batam has ceased to be a subsidiary of Rich Capital Holdings.

data.title: Bridgestone 1Q Americas Adjusted Operating Profit Y39.79B Vs Y36.8B a Year Earlier
Yida China Logs 151 Million Yuan in April Contracted Sales
data.description:
Yida China recorded contracted sales of around 151 million yuan in April, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Gross floor area stood at approximately 13,112 square meters, while the average selling price was 11,530 yuan per square meter.

From January to April, contracted sales and GFA reached roughly 323 million yuan and 27,733 square meters, respectively, with an average selling price of 11,654 yuan per square meter.

data.title: Bridgestone 1Q APAC Ex-Japan Adjusted Operating Profit Y14.60B Vs Y13.30B a Year Earlier
Geo Energy Resources' Profit Jumps 63% in Q1; Shares Slump 8%
data.description:
Geo Energy Resources' profit for the first quarter of the year surged 63% to $14.1 million from $8.7 million a year earlier, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Gross profit was up 71% to $28.5 million from $16.7 million a year earlier.

Revenue zoomed 68% year over year to $166.4 million from $99.0 million a year earlier, due to higher coal sales volume.

The company declared an interim dividend of SG$0.0025 per share.

Shares of the coal mining and exploration company slumped nearly 8% in recent trading.

data.title: France's Engie posts slight rise in first-quarter profit
data.description:
French utility Engie posted a 0.5% rise in first-quarter earnings on Thursday, as weaker hydropower conditions and lower power prices weighed on gains in its transmission business.

The company, which produces, transports and sells gas and electricity, said earnings before interest and tax (EBIT), excluding nuclear, came to 3.7 billion euros ($4.14 billion), up 0.5% compared to the same period a year earlier.

That was slightly lower than analyst estimates of 3.8 billion euros, according to a consensus gathered by LSEG.

($1 = 0.8932 euros)

data.title: Douglas Q2 core profit falls 16%, hit by weak consumer sentiment
data.description:
German premium beauty retailer Douglas reported a 16% fall in second-quarter adjusted core profit on Thursday, citing continuing weak consumer sentiment in its key markets.

Adjusted earnings before income, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) came in at 122.4 million euros ($136.93 million), down from 145.9 million euros in the same period last year.

($1 = 0.8939 euros)

data.title: Japan to seek third round of US trade talks next week, sources say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Date of Akazawa's visit fluid, depend on staff-level talks
  • Finance minister may meet US counterpart next week in Canada
  • Japan open to buy more U.S. corn, soy, shipbuilding cooperation
  • Deep gap remains over exemptions to US car, auto parts tariffs

Japan's top trade negotiator, Ryosei Akazawa, could travel to Washington as soon as next week for a third round of trade talks with the U.S., two sources with knowledge of the plans told Reuters on Thursday.

The date of his visit was fluid and would depend on how much progress the two countries can make in narrowing differences in staff-level negotiations, one of the sources said on condition of anonymity as he was not authorised to speak publicly.

Finance Minister Katsunobu Kato is also scheduled to visit Canada next week for a meeting with his G7 counterparts, where he may discuss foreign exchange with U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

Japan is considering a package of proposals to gain U.S. concessions that may include increased imports of U.S. corn and soy, technical cooperation in shipbuilding, and revision to inspection standards for imported automobiles, the source said.

There is uncertainty on whether the two sides can iron out differences over Japan's priority, which is to win exemptions from U.S. tariffs on automobile and auto parts - the mainstay of its export-heavy economy.

"We will seek reviews of the range of U.S. tariff measures, such as those on automobiles, auto parts, steel, aluminium as well as the reciprocal tariffs, that are deeply regrettable," Akazawa, who is also economic revitalisation minister, told a news conference on Wednesday.

On April 2, U.S. President Donald Trump imposed 10% tariffs on all countries except Canada, Mexico and China, along with higher tariff rates for many big trading partners, including Japan, which faces a 24% tariff rate starting in July unless it can negotiate a deal with the U.S.

Japanese policymakers and ruling party lawmakers have said they see no merit in striking a deal with the U.S. unless a 25% tariff on automobile imports is lifted, given the industry's economic importance.

The impact is already evident in the sector. Mazda reported a 45.1% drop in net profit for the fiscal year that ended in March, and held off disclosing earnings estimates for the current year through March 2026.

During his second visit to Washington on May 1, Akazawa made a request to Bessent to review the 25% automobile and auto parts tariffs, though the two sides could not reach common ground.

Initial hope of a quick deal, such as one timed around a summit of G7 advanced economies in June, is fading. Domestic media have reported that Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba is now aiming for an agreement by early July - ahead of an upper house election slated later in the month.

While Japan was the first major economy to start bilateral trade talks with the U.S., Britain was the first to strike a deal with the Trump administration. China also agreed on a truce with the U.S. in a de-escalation of trade tensions that led to a sharp rebound in global stock prices.

"Slapping tariffs on Japan is less painful for the U.S. than doing so on China. As such, the U.S. doesn't have a huge incentive to compromise and seek an early agreement with Japan," said Takahide Kiuchi, executive economist at Nomura Research Institute, predicting that bilateral talks will drag on.

Japan's upper house election also gives Ishiba little room for compromise in politically sensitive areas like agriculture. Ruling party heavyweight Hiroshi Moriyama, who is a close aide of Ishiba, has ruled out boosting imports of U.S. rice.

data.title: Cyprus rethinks EU green taxes to avoid double whammy on water, fuel
data.description:
Cyprus wants to postpone or limit new carbon taxes this year to avert a double hit to consumers from tariffs on water and fuel, President Nikos Christodoulides told television station Omega TV in an interview.

The new levies on water and fuel bills are expected as part of a so-called Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF) championed by the European Union to speed a green transition in the bloc.

"This thing some people are saying about a green tax storm - it's not happening," Christodoulides said in the remarks aired on Wednesday, adding that Nicosia was in talks with the EU on the matter.

"We are ready to lose money from this fund in order to avoid imposing increases on the public."

Cyprus's RRF, approved in 2021, has a total budget of 1.2 billion euros ($1.34 billion).

($1=0.8932 euros)

data.title: Bridgestone 1Q EMEA Adjusted Operating Profit Y9.07B Vs Y5.40B a Year Earlier
Belgian bank KBC first-quarter profit beats estimates
data.description:
Belgian bank KBC Group reported a 8% rise in first-quarter net profit that came above market expectations on Thursday, driven by solid revenue from net interest, fee and commissions, and its insurance business.

Its profit reached 546 million euros ($611.30 million) in the quarter, beating analysts' average estimate of 529 million euros in a poll compiled by the lender.

The bank updated its dividend and capital deployment policy, stating that it plans to pay a dividend corresponding to 50% to 60% of its consolidated earnings, with an interim dividend of one euro per share to be paid this November.

"The focus will predominantly be on further organic growth alongside mergers and acquisitions. We see a 13% unfloored fully loaded common equity ratio as the minimum", Johan Thijs, CEO of KBC said in a statement, referring to the outlook for capital deployment.

At end of the quarter the ratio came at 14.5%.

The bank has also announced a signing agreement to buy Slovak lender 365.bank for 761 million euros.

($1 = 0.8932 euros)

data.title: Akzo Nobel India's Consolidated Profit Slips in Fiscal Q4
data.description:
Akzo Nobel India witnessed a marginal decline in its consolidated attributable profit to 1.08 billion rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, against 1.09 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share contracted to 23.80 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 23.87 rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the paints and coating manufacturer.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4, however, increased to 10.2 billion rupees from 9.7 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board recommended a final dividend of 30 rupees per equity share for the financial year 2024-25.

Akzo Nobel's share prices were down over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Sanwa Holdings' Profit Jumps 33% in Fiscal 2024
data.description:
Sanwa Holdings profit attributable to owners of the parent jumped 33% to 57.5 billion yen in the fiscal year ended March 31, from 43.2 billion yen a year ago.

The metal window and door manufacturer said diluted earnings per share climbed to 264.40 yen from 195.56 yen, according to a Wednesday filing with the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Net sales gained 8.4% to 662.4 billion yen from 611.1 billion yen a year earlier.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 59.00 yen per share, bringing the full-year dividend to 106.00 yen per share. Payments will commence on June 26.

For fiscal 2025, the company expects attributable profit of 58.0 billion, an EPS of 270.70 yen, while revenue is projected to reach 654 billion yen.

The company plans to pay total dividend of 124.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

data.title: Fujairah Cement Industries Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Fujairah Cement Industries (ADX:FCI) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi bourse on Thursday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started on Wednesday and was lifted after the cement company published the results of its meeting.
